description_long: This is a weathered, rectangular metal street cabinet mounted on a concrete base, commonly found in urban environments for housing electrical or communication equipment. The cabinet is painted a faded blue and is heavily covered with graffiti, stickers, and handwritten messages in various languages, giving it a distinctly urban, vandalized appearance. The front features two main doors with visible hinges and a central seam, as well as several warning and informational stickers. The sides are narrower, also marked with graffiti, and one side displays a large, crude drawing. The back is similarly covered in graffiti, including a large dinosaur sketch and more handwritten notes. The cabinet's surface is matte and shows signs of rust, scratches, and general wear, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure.
description: A blue, metal street cabinet on a concrete base, covered in graffiti and stickers, with visible wear and rust.
description_view_0: Front view showing two main doors, multiple stickers, and dense graffiti.
description_view_1: Narrow side view with graffiti and a few stickers, less surface area visible.
description_view_2: Back view with extensive graffiti, including a large dinosaur drawing and handwritten notes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with graffiti, a sticker, and a large crude drawing.
